See also[edit]

Supplement to the S² Works Appendix includes listing of all tracks present in the series, including those not released in the S² Works.

References[edit]

  1. ↑ 1.0 1.1 1.2 These songs appear in the same form for each of their respective versions of every episode. As such, they will only be listed for the first episode, and none beyond that.
  2. ↑ This piece of music is plays while Shinji is on hold waiting for Gendo. Whether it falls into the category of music or SFX is debatable, but it certainly is not a piece to be found on any released album.
  3. ↑ Here, Death(True)² is treated as the default version. In any case, the music in all versions is nearly identical (the only exception is noted below).
  4. ↑ 4.0 4.1 These two songs only appear in the theatrical version of Death. Since that version of the film is all but impossible to see, the placement of these two songs with respect to chronology is hard to pinpoint, and more speculative than anything.
  5. ↑ Rebirth and Episode 25' are in the same section because they essentially exist as two versions of the same episode. Songs present in only one version are noted with the corresponding title, and songs present in both are unmarked. After Soul's Refrain, all the remaining music is 25' only, and as such, is left unmarked.
  6. ↑ This track begins earlier in the theatrical version of Rebirth than it does in Episode 25'

External Links[edit]

  • EvaGeeks Forums. The Editorium. "Music in Chronological Order of Usage Updates and Notes".

references.content: These songs appear in the same form for each of their respective versions of every episode. As such, they will only be listed for the first episode, and none beyond that.

This piece of music is plays while Shinji is on hold waiting for Gendo. Whether it falls into the category of music or SFX is debatable, but it certainly is not a piece to be found on any released album.
Here, Death(True)² is treated as the default version. In any case, the music in all versions is nearly identical (the only exception is noted below).

These two songs only appear in the theatrical version of Death. Since that version of the film is all but impossible to see, the placement of these two songs with respect to chronology is hard to pinpoint, and more speculative than anything.

Rebirth and Episode 25' are in the same section because they essentially exist as two versions of the same episode. Songs present in only one version are noted with the corresponding title, and songs present in both are unmarked. After Soul's Refrain, all the remaining music is 25' only, and as such, is left unmarked.
This track begins earlier in the theatrical version of Rebirth than it does in Episode 25'
